name: Core Shlokas for Purnima (Full Moon) Worship

shlokas.name: Chandra Arghya — Offering Water to the Full Moon
shlokas.purpose: The primary Purnima ritual — offering water (Arghya) to the moon while chanting this mantra
shlokas.when_to_chant: On Purnima evening when the full moon rises — stand facing the moon and pour water toward it
shlokas.sanskrit:
ॐ चन्द्रं गच्छ महाभाग चन्द्र सोम शुभप्रद ।
चन्द्रकान्त महाभाग गृहाणार्घ्यं नमोऽस्तु ते ॥
shlokas.iast:
oṃ candraṃ gaccha mahābhāga candra soma śubhaprada |
candrakānta mahābhāga gṛhāṇārghyaṃ namo'stu te ||
shlokas.english_meaning: O Moon of great fortune — O Chandra, O Soma, O giver of auspiciousness — O the beloved of the moon (moonstone), O greatly fortunate one — accept this water offering. Salutations to You.

word_meanings.soma: Soma — the moon deity, also the divine nectar. The moon 'pours' Soma nectar that nourishes all life.
word_meanings.arghya: water offering — held in cupped hands and poured toward the moon

shlokas.how_to_perform: Fill a copper vessel or cupped hands with water. After moonrise, stand outdoors facing the moon. Pour the water slowly in the direction of the moon while chanting. The water catches the moonlight as it falls — this is the offering.

shlokas.name: Chandra Stotra — Praise of the Moon
shlokas.purpose: Complete hymn to the Moon deity — recited on Purnima especially on Sharad Purnima (Kojagari) and Kartika Purnima
shlokas.when_to_chant: On Purnima evenings, especially Sharad Purnima and Kartika Purnima; also on Somavar (Monday) evenings

verses.title: The moon as Soma — nourisher of all life
verses.sanskrit:
देवानां च ऋषीणां च गणानाञ्च सुपर्वणाम् ।
उदयस्त्वमुपायासि यज्ञानां पतिरेव च ॥
verses.iast:
devānāṃ ca ṛṣīṇāṃ ca gaṇānāñca suparvaṇām |
udayastvamupāyāsi yajñānāṃ patireva ca ||
verses.english_meaning: You rise as the protector of gods, sages, and all auspicious groups. You are indeed the master of all sacrifices (yajnas).

verses.title: The moon as physician
verses.sanskrit:
ओषधीनां प्रभुर्देव रोहिण्याः पतिरेव च ।
सुधाकरो महाभाग सुधांशो मृगलाञ्छन ॥
verses.iast:
oṣadhīnāṃ prabhurdeva rohiṇyāḥ patireva ca |
sudhākaro mahābhāga sudhāṃśo mṛgalāñchana ||
verses.english_meaning: O Lord of medicinal herbs, O husband of Rohini (the moon's favorite wife among the 27 nakshatras), O great-fortuned, O possessor of nectar, O moon marked with the deer (the dark shadow on the moon's surface).
verses.significance: The moon rules all herbs and medicinal plants in Ayurveda. Herbs are best collected at the full moon when their potency is highest. The 'deer' in the moon is the dark shadow visible to the naked eye — a beloved traditional image.

verses.title: The moon who causes tides — and emotions
verses.sanskrit:
जगतः पितरं नित्यं सर्वग्रहगणेश्वरम् ।
नमस्कृत्य प्रसन्नास्मि सुखीभव जगत्पते ॥
verses.iast:
jagataḥ pitaraṃ nityaṃ sarvagrahagaṇeśvaram |
namaskṛtya prasannāsmi sukhībhava jagatpate ||
verses.english_meaning: Bowing to the eternal father of the world, the lord of all the group of planets — may you be pleased, O master of the universe. Be happy (be pleased).
verses.significance: The moon called 'father of the world' — because the lunar calendar and lunar rhythms structure the months, festivals, and agricultural cycles of all of Hindu culture. Life flows with the moon.

shlokas.name: Purnima Satyanarayan Vrat Sankalpa
shlokas.purpose: Many families perform Satyanarayan Puja on Purnima. This sankalpa is for that combination.
shlokas.when_to_chant: On Purnima, before performing Satyanarayan Puja
shlokas.english_meaning: For my highest welfare, I perform the Satyanarayan Puja on this Purnima.

shlokas.name: Kojagari Purnima — Lakshmi Night Worship Mantra
shlokas.purpose: Special for Sharad Purnima (Kojagari) — Lakshmi descends to earth on this night asking 'who is awake?'
shlokas.when_to_chant: On Sharad Purnima (Ashwin Purnima) night — especially after midnight
shlokas.sanskrit:
को जागर्ति को जागर्ति भक्त्या भजत मां सदा ।
धनं धान्यं ददाम्येषां भक्तानां वाञ्छितं फलम् ॥
shlokas.iast:
ko jāgarti ko jāgarti bhaktyā bhajata māṃ sadā |
dhanaṃ dhānyaṃ dadāmyeṣāṃ bhaktānāṃ vāñchitaṃ phalam ||
shlokas.english_meaning: Who is awake? Who is awake? Worship Me with devotion always. I give wealth and grain, and the desired fruit to my devoted ones.
shlokas.significance: Kojagari = 'Ko Jagarti?' (Who is awake?). On Sharad Purnima night, Lakshmi walks the earth asking who is awake in her name. Those who stay awake through the night, worshipping her, are said to receive her direct blessing — wealth, health, and fulfilment.
shlokas.how_to_observe: Stay awake through Kojagari Purnima night. Place a pot of milk and rice pudding (kheer) under the moonlight — moonlight on this night is said to have healing and nourishing properties. Offer the moonlit kheer to Lakshmi and distribute as prasad.

shlokas.name: Kartika Purnima — Deepdaan (Lamp Offering) Mantra
shlokas.purpose: Kartika Purnima is the most sacred Purnima — Tripura Sundari festival, Pushkar fair, and the night Dev Diwali (gods' Diwali). Lamps are floated on water.
shlokas.when_to_chant: On Kartika Purnima evening, while floating a diya on water or placing it on the banks of a river/lake
shlokas.sanskrit:
कार्तिक पूर्णिमायाम् दीपदानं करोमि ।
त्रिपुरान्तकाय नमः । देव दीपावलि नमस्ते ॥
shlokas.iast:
kārtika pūrṇimāyāṃ dīpadānaṃ karomi |
tripurāntakāya namaḥ | deva dīpāvali namaste ||
shlokas.english_meaning: On Kartika Purnima, I offer this lamp. Salutations to Shiva who destroyed Tripura (the triple demon cities). Salutations to Dev Diwali.
shlokas.significance: Kartika Purnima celebrates Shiva's victory over the three demon cities (Tripura) — he destroyed all three with a single arrow. The gods celebrated with lamps, hence 'Dev Diwali' — the Diwali of the gods, held exactly 15 days after the human Diwali (Kartika Amavasya). Lighting lamps on water on this night is one of the most ancient Hindu rituals.
